items.body_text: The status of Cleveland's signal-callers looms large as we look at what's still up in the air this offseason.

items.title: Free agent Diggs: No WR2 in NFL better than me
items.body_text: Free agent Stefon Diggs says he is better than any No. 2 wide receiver currently on an NFL roster.

items.title: Niners part owner agrees to purchase Seahawks
items.body_text: A group led by Niners limited partner Vinod Khosla has agreed to purchase the Seahawks, with sources telling ESPN the selling price will be a record $9.612 billion. The Khosla family will be required to divest its interest in the 49ers.
items.author: Seth Wickersham and Adam Schefter

items.title: No GPS tracker for Arnold, can work out for teams
items.body_text: Former Lions cornerback Terrion Arnold does not need to wear a GPS tracker as a condition of his release, a Florida judge ruled Friday, allowing him to continue to work out with NFL teams in an effort to get re-signed after being cut.
items.author: Xuan Thai

items.title: Study: NFLers die from dementia at higher rate
items.body_text: NFL players are four times more likely than the general population to die of neurodegenerative diseases such as dementia and Parkinson's, according to a study released Wednesday.
items.author: Robert Klemko

items.title: Commanders to retire No. 44 of HOF RB Riggins
items.body_text: The Commanders are retiring the No. 44 worn by Hall of Famer and franchise rushing leader John Riggins -- more than 40 years after one of the most famous runs in Super Bowl history.
items.author: John Keim

items.title: Clutch Vinatieri to enter Colts' Ring of Honor
items.body_text: Kicker Adam Vinatieri, who will enter the Pro Football Hall of Fame next month, will enter the Colts' Ring of Honor during a ceremony Oct. 18.
items.author: Stephen Holder

items.title: Late Cowboys' player Kneeland diagnosed with CTE
items.body_text: Former Cowboys defensive end Marshawn Kneeland has been posthumously diagnosed with CTE.

items.title: Sources: CB Douglas to sign with Commanders
items.body_text: Free agent CB Rasul Douglas is planning to sign a one-year deal with the Washington Commanders worth up to $3.8 million, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter.
